body.p1: HOBC uses the HobbyHash Coin name, the HOBC ticker, and the `hobc` address identity. It is a SHA-256 coin with mainnet node software, local RPC, mining pools, public APIs, and documentation built around one clear brand.
body.p2: The supply plan is simple: 84,000,000 HOBC target supply, 8,400,000 HOBC launch reserve, and 75,600,000 HOBC normal mining target. Block 1 is the special launch reserve block, and normal mining starts after that under the documented subsidy rules.
body.p3: The website is the HOBC command center. It brings together the home dashboard, mining pages, main and nano pool setup, explorer, stats, downloads, docs, launch reserve information, burn tracker, FAQ, contact, and wallet pages.
body.p4: Every live value should come from real APIs, real local node RPC, real pool status files, real wallet database state, or clearly marked pending data. If something is not ready, the website should say syncing, offline, pending launch, or not available.
body.p5: HOBC pools are solo only. Shares prove that a miner is working, but payouts happen only when a worker finds a real block. The payout address is the HOBC address before the dot in the worker name.
body.p6: HOBC offers a custodial web wallet and an official Windows standalone wallet. The web wallet is convenient for deposits and withdrawals, but the website controls those keys and funds until a withdrawal is broadcast. The Windows standalone wallet installs on your PC and keeps keys local. Use the web wallet for smaller balances, and the Windows standalone wallet for larger balances and long-term storage.
body.p7: Wallet deposits require real confirmations. Withdrawals must create real on-chain txids. Public wallet pages must never expose private wallet data or public user balances. Download the Windows standalone wallet from the official downloads page when you want local custody instead of the web wallet.
card.no_fake_data.body: HOBC pages must not fake market price, market cap, blocks, wallet balances, txids, burns, explorer results, or pool stats. Unavailable data must be labeled honestly.
card.no_fake_data.title: No Fake Data
card.transparency.body: Launch reserve and burn tracking must be based on real chain data. Reserve tracking can be pending, but it should not be replaced with guessed balances or fake txids.
card.transparency.title: Transparency
card.where_we_are_going.body: The build direction is to keep the chain, pools, explorer, wallet, docs, reserve, burn tracker, and services organized around a clean HOBC launch path for real users.
card.where_we_are_going.title: Where We Are Going
hero.lead: HobbyHash Coin (HOBC) is a SHA-256 coin and public command center built for home solo miners, transparent chain information, simple pool setup, and honest website status.
hero.title: HobbyHash Coin
meta.description: About HobbyHash Coin, the HOBC website, and the home solo mining mission.
meta.title: About HOBC
notice.1: Project goal: HOBC is meant to make solo mining approachable without pretending that mining is guaranteed income. The website should help users run a node, mine to their own HOBC address, understand custody risk, and verify public data without fake prices, fake blocks, fake txids, or fake balances.
